l1: Are replication delays occurring globally or only between specific physical sites?
When did clients start connecting to domain controllers outside their local site?
Are all subnet ranges currently associated with a corresponding Active Directory site?
l2: Is the Inter-Site Topology Generator (ISTG) failing to create necessary connection objects?
Are bridgehead servers overloaded or manually assigned, blocking automatic failover?
Has a change in network routing tables rendered site link costs incorrect or obsolete?

l1: Run 'repadmin /kcc' to force the Knowledge Consistency Checker to run on the local DC.
Execute 'repadmin /istg' to identify the current ISTG owner for the site.
Check the Directory Service event log for Event IDs 1311 or 1566 topology generation failures.
l2: Run 'repadmin /showconn *' to view all automatically and manually created connection objects.
Execute a PowerShell query to match local IP subnets against the NTDS site mappings configurations.
Use 'nltest /dsaddress:<Client_IP>' to see exactly which site a specific target resolves to.

l1: Associate unassigned subnets to their proper geographical location via ADSS console.
Clear forced or broken manual connection objects to allow KCC to build optimal pathways.
Verify that RPC dynamic ports are open between all designated site bridgehead servers.
l2: Enable or disable the 'Bridge all site links' setting depending on network mesh design rules.
Re-allocate FSMO or ISTG roles if the current owner server has become chronically unresponsive.
Adjust site link costs and replication intervals to align with actual hardware link speeds.

l1: Export all subnet-to-site associations to a text layout via Active Directory PowerShell module.
Collect comprehensive KCC execution logs by grabbing recent Directory Service event traces.
Save the output of 'repadmin /failcache' to log persistent cross-site connection blocks.
l2: Export the entire configuration partition site topology tree structure using LDP or LDIFDE.
Capture packet sweeps during forced KCC runs over port 135 and dynamic RPC ranges.
Gather target router IP translation tables to isolate network address translation anomalies.

missing_subnet_site_mapping.title: Resolving Cross-Site Authentication Traversal Caused by Missing Subnet Maps

steps.expect: Returns a DC situated in a remote datacenter rather than the local branch office server node
Log entry shows client IP address connected without any matching subnet registration in ADSS configuration entries
Command successfully creates and binds the subnet to the appropriate site object container
Instantly resolves to the local subnet domain controller node, updating client site properties cleanly
